ambiguities.aspect: Allocation of purchase price between land and building
ambiguities.input_contextualized_ifrs_default: IFRS requires allocation of lump-sum purchase price based on relative fair values — land $8M and building $4M totaling $12M, so land = 8/12 × $9M = $6M and building = 4/12 × $9M = $3M.

ambiguities.aspect: Sales tax treatment on building portion
ambiguities.input_contextualized_ifrs_default: Buyer-side recoverable tax on purchases is an asset (Input Tax Credit / Tax assets) — 10% sales tax on building ($3M) = $300,000 recorded as tax asset.

ambiguities.input_contextualized_conventional_defaul: 'paid by cheque' resolves as cash payment; 50% due in 6 months resolves as accounts payable liability.

ambiguities.aspect: Whether 6-month payment requires present value discounting
ambiguities.input_contextualized_ifrs_default: Short-term payment plans (6 months or less within normal credit terms) resolve at face value — no discounting required.

best_attempt.reason: Allocate $9M purchase price based on relative fair values: Land = (8/12) × $9M = $6M; Building = (4/12) × $9M = $3M; Sales tax on building = 10% × $3M = $300,000; 50% paid now = $4.5M + $300,000 tax = $4.8M; 50% payable = $4.5M.

output_decision_maker.proceed_reason: IFRS relative fair value allocation, recoverable tax treatment, and short-term payment at face value rules resolve all aspects definitively.
output_decision_maker.overall_final_rationale: All aspects are resolved by IFRS rules (relative fair value allocation, recoverable tax as asset, short-term payable at face value) and conventional terms (paid by cheque = cash, balance due = payable), with no ambiguities or capability gaps.
